dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the betrayal goes deeper than we thought. The trade representative wasn't acting alone. There's a network of compromised officials.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found correspondence showing that three other settlement leaders have been making side deals with the {enemy_type}. The stolen plans were just one piece. {quest_ally} says the whole {quest_focus} was rotten from the start: people swearing oaths they never intended to keep. {quest_item} contains the full ledger of bribes.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {quest_item} from {location} before the conspirators destroy it. The partner settlement needs to see the full picture: not just one traitor, but a pattern. Only then can real trust be rebuilt.

playerResponses.text: Rot this deep needs to be cut out completely. I'll get the ledger.
Are you sure exposing everything won't make things worse?
This conspiracy is bigger than one person can handle.

dialogueChunks.intro: The guard captain knows someone is investigating. She's moved up the watch rotation and restricted access to the garrison records.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} tried to access the patrol logs and was turned away at the door. The captain is burning documents: {quest_ally} saw smoke from the records room chimney at midnight. If she destroys {quest_item}, the proof of {quest_threat} goes up in smoke. The next shift change happens at {location}.
dialogueChunks.outro: We have a narrow window to get {quest_item} out of the garrison before the captain finishes covering her tracks. The guards loyal to her won't let anyone through the front. There might be another way in.

playerResponses.text: If she's burning evidence, we're running out of time. Show me the other way in.
Confronting her directly might force a confession.

dialogueChunks.intro: The person who forged the charter has been identified. It's the settlement clerk: the same person everyone trusted to maintain the records. And they've fled to {location}.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} discovered that the clerk has been altering documents for years, not just the charter but trade agreements, boundary markers, and inheritance records. Always benefiting the same family. {quest_ally} found the original charter hidden in a wall cavity, but the clerk took {quest_item}: a seal that authenticates official documents.
dialogueChunks.outro: Without {quest_item}, even the real charter can be questioned. The clerk took it to {location}, probably planning to sell it or destroy it. Track them down and recover the seal before {quest_threat} becomes permanent.

playerResponses.text: A clerk who betrays a community's trust is the worst kind of thief. I'll find them.
What happens to the clerk when I catch them?
They're probably long gone by now.
